title: If graph G is NOT connected and has 35 edges, then it must have
content:
If graph G is NOT connected and has 35 edges, then it must have at least ____ vertices. @[](3)

A. 7
B. 8
C. 9
D. 10
author: 陈越
description: If graph G is NOT connected and has 35 edges, then it must have at least ____ vertices.

title: A graph with 90 vertices and 20 edges must have at most __ conne
content:
A graph with 90 vertices and 20 edges must have at most __ connected component(s). @[](2)

A. 69
B. 70
C. 84
D. 85

title: Given an undirected graph G with 16 edges, where 3 vertices are
content:
Given an undirected graph G with 16 edges, where 3 vertices are of degree 4, 4 vertices are of degree 3, and all the other vertices are of degrees less than 3.  Then G must have at least __ vertices. @[](4)

A. 10
B. 11
C. 13
D. 15
author: 考研试卷
description: Given an undirected graph G with 16 edges, where 3 vertices are of degree 4, 4 vertices are of degree 3, and all the other vertices are of degrees less than 3.  Then G must have at least __ vertices.

title: Hamiltonian Cycle
content:
The "Hamilton cycle problem" is to find a simple cycle that contains every vertex in a graph. Such a cycle is called a "Hamiltonian cycle".

In this problem, you are supposed to tell if a given cycle is a Hamiltonian cycle.

### Input Specification:

Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line contains 2 positive integers $$N$$ ($$2< N \le 200$$), the number of vertices, and $$M$$, the number of edges in an undirected graph. Then $$M$$ lines follow, each describes an edge in the format `Vertex1 Vertex2`, where the vertices are numbered from 1 to $$N$$. The next line gives a positive integer $$K$$ which is the number of queries, followed by $$K$$ lines of queries, each in the format:

$$n$$ $$V_1$$ $$V_2$$ ... $$V_n$$

where $$n$$ is the number of vertices in the list, and $$V_i$$'s are the vertices on a path.

### Output Specification:

For each query, print in a line `YES` if the path does form a Hamiltonian cycle, or `NO` if not.

### Sample Input:
```in
6 10
6 2
3 4
1 5
2 5
3 1
4 1
1 6
6 3
1 2
4 5
6
7 5 1 4 3 6 2 5
6 5 1 4 3 6 2
9 6 2 1 6 3 4 5 2 6
4 1 2 5 1
7 6 1 3 4 5 2 6
7 6 1 2 5 4 3 1
```

### Sample Output:
```out
YES
NO
NO
NO
YES
NO
```
author: CHEN, Yue
